scope_boundary: finite authority-record proof and synthetic fixture-gate behavior only; no deployed authorization enforcement, runtime adapter behavior, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has exact source mappings for the System Boundaries claim, a synthetic authority-transition harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures, and a stronger finite Lean authority decision envelope for allow, deny, and escalate records. Those artifacts support the design discipline but still do not prove deployed permission enforcement, runtime adapter safety, revocation propagation, live confused-deputy resistance, or semantic adequacy of the broader authority model.
required_artifacts: revocation propagation tests across delegated and replacement contexts
adversarial confused-deputy probes against live tool wrappers
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_authority_transitions.py && (cd lean && lake build) && python3 scripts/validate_proof_artifact_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: Authority Lean predicates are finite-record predicates
runtime adapter enforcement and revocation propagation are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: claim text interpreted as deployed enforcement
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the specific artifact lane that actually passed, or after deployed permission enforcement, revocation propagation, and confused-deputy behavior are tested and reviewed with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: finite Lean records do not prove runtime enforcement
synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed tool-wrapper behavior
source mappings support architectural discussion but do not by themselves promote support state
no independent security or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent security, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote System Boundaries above argument
does not prove deployed authorization enforcement
does not prove runtime adapter safety
does not prove confused-deputy resistance in a live system
